text.title: Competitor Tracker Template (Solo) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Competitor Tracker Template (Solo) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each competitor row pins ≥3 named channels watched. Generic 'their website' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without specific channels operators miss launches that don't hit the homepage.

rule.statement: Weekly scan logs DELTAS, not current state. State snapshots are kept monthly; weekly logs only what changed.
rule.rationale: State snapshots flood the log; delta logs surface what matters.

rule.statement: Each delta entry MUST carry a triage classification (ignore / log / queue / urgent). Untriaged deltas are rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Untriaged deltas produce decision paralysis; classification forces a routing decision.

rule.statement: Monthly matrix MUST be refreshed at month-end. Stale matrices (>45 days) are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Without refresh cadence the matrix becomes folklore.

rule.statement: Any delta classified 'queue' or 'urgent' MUST link to a roadmap item or decision-record. Loose 'queue' entries with no link are rejected.
rule.rationale: Queue without link rots into ignored backlog.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
